competitors.name: Snyk
competitors.status: IPO uncertain, growth ~12%. Reachability expanded Mar 9. Embracing Claude Code Security.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: Full CTEM lifecycle vs. SCA+SAST only. Multi-LLM consensus vs. single model. 97% noise reduction across ALL scanners vs SCA reachability only.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: Reachability improvements for JS/Java/Python (Mar 9). Package Health Check for agentic dev. IPO crossroads.

competitors.name: Wiz
competitors.status: Google acquisition closing MID-MARCH 2026. EU approved. DOJ cleared. Orca lawsuit settled (Jan 2026). CISPE alarmed.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: Scanner-neutral Switzerland vs. Google lock-in. AppSec-focused vs. cloud-only. Air-gapped capable. PEAK Switzerland moment NOW.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: Closing mid-March. Orca lawsuit dismissed with prejudice. Clean entry to Google.

competitors.name: Semgrep
competitors.status: Growing. Series D. RSA 2026 Booth #1743. AI Detection product launch. Opengrep fork by 10 companies.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: Multi-LLM consensus (3+ models) vs. single-model 95%. Full CTEM lifecycle vs. SAST-only. MPTE verification.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: RSA 2026 presence. AI Detection product. 75M scans, 740K autofixes in 2025.

competitors.name: Checkmarx
competitors.status: AWS Kiro IDE integration. Sale process stalled. Directly threatened by Claude Code Security.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: Enterprise-wide Decision Intelligence vs IDE-only. Air-gapped capability. Vendor stability (no sale uncertainty).
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: AWS Kiro IDE integration. Tromzo agents launching as Checkmarx Assist. Sale still stalled.

competitors.name: Endor Labs
competitors.status: Acquired Autonomous Plane (Feb 2026). RSA 2026 exhibitor. Rising in Cyber.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: 97% noise reduction across ALL scanner types (not just SCA). MPTE verification vs. static analysis. Full CTEM lifecycle.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: Autonomous Plane acquisition for full-stack reachability. 6 OpenClaw zero-days. ArmorCode integration.

competitors.name: CrowdStrike
competitors.status: FalconID GA. Fal.Con Gov Mar 18. SGNL ($740M) + Seraphic ($420M) acquired.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: AppSec-focused vs. endpoint-first. CTEM lifecycle vs. detection-only. Developer-centric. Air-gapped AI capability.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: FalconID phishing-resistant MFA GA. Fal.Con Gov Mar 18 with White House, CISA, DoW leaders.

competitors.name: Orca Security
competitors.status: Patent lawsuit vs Wiz settled (lost 3/6 patents at PTAB). Mindshare declining (4.0%→2.6%). 495 employees.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: MPTE proves exploitability vs. static analysis. Full AppSec vs. cloud-workload-only. Growing vs. declining mindshare.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: Wiz lawsuit settled. PTAB invalidated 3/6 patents. Mindshare declining.

competitors.name: ArmorCode
competitors.status: Closest ASPM competitor. MCP server validates V7. Endor Labs SCA integration.
competitors.aldeci_advantage: Native scanners (they aggregate only). MPTE verification. Multi-LLM consensus. Air-gapped capable.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: Endor Labs SCA integration. MCP server validates V7 thesis.

competitors.name: Cyera
competitors.status: Most heavily funded cybersecurity company. Different category (DSPM vs. CTEM).
competitors.aldeci_advantage: Different market (DSPM vs. CTEM). Potential integration partner.
competitors.latest_update: 2026-03-02: No new updates. Valuation benchmark for AI-native security.

trends: BREAKING: Anthropic blacklisted by Pentagon — Claude hits #1 App Store, OpenAI strikes Pentagon deal
Single-LLM-provider dependency is now GEOPOLITICAL risk — one executive order can cut access
Wiz-Google $32B closing mid-March 2026 — creates Switzerland opportunity for independent vendors
SGLang at 400K+ GPUs — becoming de facto LLM inference standard globally
WebMCP in Chrome early preview — browser-native MCP integration emerging
NIST CAISI agentic AI security RFI (deadline Mar 9) — regulation catching up to agentic AI
RSA 2026 (Mar 23-26) — biggest cybersecurity conference, agentic AI is dominant theme
AI arms race: Claude Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, GPT-5.3-Codex all launched same period
OpenAI market share declining (62%→53%) — validates multi-model approach
Open-weight cybersecurity LLMs emerging (Trend Micro Llama 3, Cisco collab)
Agentic AI is the defining theme of 2026 — every vendor embedding autonomous agents
MCP (Model Context Protocol) becoming dominant standard (97M SDK downloads/month)
Platform consolidation via aggressive M&A (477 deals projected for 2026, record pace)
Sovereign M&A — US vendors acquiring EU firms for NIS2/CRA compliance
AI-generated code security emerging as new category
Claude Code Security disrupted traditional SAST/DAST — reasoning vulnerability hunting
AI adversary operations up 89% YoY (CrowdStrike 2026 Threat Report)
27-second eCrime breakout — automated remediation now critical
CrowdStrike FalconID — endpoint vendors moving into identity security

Post-quantum cryptography entering production (Google HTTPS PQC, NIST ML-KEM)
1,339 CVEs with >90% exploitation probability in EPSS
Wiz-Orca patent battle settled — PTAB invalidated 3/6 Orca patents
Premium valuations for AI-native security startups vs. non-AI peers
Opengrep fork of Semgrep CE — community trust in open-source under strain
differentiators: Geopolitical resilience — multi-LLM + air-gap = no single executive order can disable security AI
8 built-in fallback scanners (works air-gapped, no external tools needed)
12-step Brain Pipeline (full CTEM lifecycle — no competitor has full loop)
Multi-LLM Consensus (3+ LLMs vote, 85% threshold — validated by Pentagon crisis)
19-phase MPTE Verification (proves exploitability, doesn't just detect)
FAIL Engine (chaos engineering for AppSec — industry first, zero competitors)
Air-gapped deployment with vLLM v0.16 + open-weight cybersecurity models ($0/mo)
AI-powered AutoFix with 10 fix types and confidence-based auto-apply
MCP-native with 705+ auto-discovered tools (first-mover in AI-native AppSec)
Scanner-neutral Switzerland positioning (critical as Wiz → Google this month)
Quantum-secure evidence (FIPS 204 ML-DSA + RSA hybrid — 5-year head start)
Claude Code Security integration — 'Claude finds, ALdeci decides' — category creation
AI agent security testing via MPTE — emerging attack surface validation
16-agent autonomous AI company model — unique investor narrative
NIST-ready agentic AI security — ahead of CAISI regulatory framework
